title: Wall Street rises driven by cooling inflation and PPI data
summary: The US Producer Price Index fell 0.3% in June, the Fed's Beige Book anticipates a moderation in prices, and Asian and US markets react with optimism.

title: SpaceX shares fall below IPO price and its bonds plummet
summary: The value of Elon Musk's space company collapses, losing more than a trillion dollars in market cap, while the yield on its bonds approaches 'junk bond' levels.

title: Fed, Microsoft and Panza fund warn of possible artificial intelligence bubble
summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh, Microsoft's CEO, and investment manager Panza warn that enthusiasm for AI could generate a bubble and a subsequent collapse in investment.

title: Stripe and Advent launch $53 billion offer to buy PayPal
summary: The joint offer of $60.50 per share raises PayPal's stock price and reflects the consolidation trend in the fintech sector.

title: Wall Street banks achieve record profits in first half driven by artificial intelligence
summary: Large US banks benefit from the AI boom and deregulation, achieving record revenues in the first half of the year.

title: ASML earns 27% more in Q2 and raises sales forecast to 45 billion
summary: The Dutch semiconductor equipment manufacturer exceeds expectations thanks to demand for AI chips.

title: Pentair plummets on stock market after disappointing results and lowering forecasts, dragging down Fluidra
summary: Pentair shares fall nearly 15% and infect Spanish Fluidra, which loses about 3% on the Ibex 35.

title: IBM sinks 25% on stock market as it loses customers redirecting spending to artificial intelligence
summary: Investors punish the traditional technology company amid the migration of IT spending towards AI solutions.

title: Chinese chip maker CXMT seeks $10 billion in largest IPO in China since 2010
summary: The semiconductor company plans to list on the Shanghai stock exchange with a valuation close to $10 billion.

title: Indra to participate in reconstruction of Ukraine's air traffic control system
summary: The Spanish technology company will collaborate in the restoration of Ukrainian air navigation infrastructure.

title: Ibex 35 extends correction and closes with contained losses on earnings day
summary: The Spanish index maintains a downward trend amid the release of corporate results.

title: European markets close mixed; DAX loses 25,000 points and Switzerland rises
summary: The German index retreats slightly while the Swiss market remains firm and the rest of Europe trades with no clear trend.

title: Market summary for July 15: six key points for investors
summary: The day was marked by US inflation data, corporate earnings, and the performance of the major global indices.

title: Geopolitical tensions threaten global supply of oil, rare earths and critical minerals
summary: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz, the dispute between the EU and China over rare earths, and insecurity in copper mines increase the risk of shortages of strategic raw materials.

title: European Commission accepts X's corrective measures to comply with Digital Services Act
summary: Elon Musk's platform commits to implementing changes to adapt to EU digital services regulations.

title: Trump's nominee to lead intelligence does not confirm that Biden won the 2020 election
summary: During his confirmation hearing, John Clayton avoids explicitly acknowledging the legitimacy of the 2020 presidential election.

title: Thames Water warns about its financial future while Manchester mayor proposes nationalization
summary: The UK's largest water company faces debt problems and Labour politician Andy Burnham proposes state control.

title: University of California Berkeley bans use of artificial intelligence in law school assignments
summary: The measure aims to foster critical thinking and independence of future lawyers from AI.

title: Bitcoin recovers from its lows after US inflation data
summary: The cryptocurrency recovers thanks to the cooling of consumer prices, which reduces pressure on the Federal Reserve.

title: BlackRock's assets under management reach a record high of $15.3 trillion
summary: The world's largest asset manager increases its assets thanks to market rallies and net capital inflows.
